{{Infobox location
| name = Myria
| introduced = House of Blades
}}

''See also: [[Myria Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Myria is a village situated on flat, sandy plains between the city of [[Enosh]] and [[Damascan]] urban centers. The settlement is protected by high walls constructed from wooden sticks tied together, which serve to deter wild animals from the nearby desert and potentially provide a defense against heretics traveling from the west. The interior of the village consists of a dense, maze-like collection of diverse houses built from clay bricks or wood. The surrounding geography features rocky terrain, small creeks, and sparse trees, though the land to the south is more fertile than the area to the north. The village has been a site of significant conflict and destruction. It was targeted during a raid involving soldiers and an [[Endross]], and it later suffered devastating damage from the [[Naraka Incarnation]], which left much of the village in ashes. During these periods of violence, the village became a battlefield where [[Travelers]] clashed with burning creatures. Despite its vulnerability due to its location, Myria is considered a part of the kingdom and has been a place where individuals have gone to study common [[life]] or seek connections to ancestral bloodlines, including the potential lineage of [[Elysia]].}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Located on flat, sandy plains near a desert and the northwestern edge of the [[Latari Forest]], Myria is a riding-accessible village. The settlement is encircled by head-high wooden walls made of sharp sticks and features a north [[gate]]. Though it currently exists as scorched ruins, the village is surrounded by rocky bluffs and sits near an entrance to [[Orgrith Cave]] large enough to hold half of its population. Myria serves as the origin for [[Alin]]'s party and was intended to be the destination for [[Simon]]'s group, representing a place to which people can be returned.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">14</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Myria]] is a place that can be reached by riding.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c1">''House of Blades'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Myria]] is a village encircled by head-high wooden walls made of sharp sticks.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2">''House of Blades'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The village of [[Myria]] is located near a desert.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Myria]] is a village with a north [[gate]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Myria]] is a village located on flat and sandy plains.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] has been in [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4">''House of Blades'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Myria]] is a village that suffered scorched ruins.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c5">''House of Blades'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Myria]] is a location.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c8">''House of Blades'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The entrance to [[Orgrith Cave]] is wide enough that half the people of [[Myria]] could fit inside.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c11">''House of Blades'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The rocky bluffs around [[Myria]] are similar to the stone walls in the chamber.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c12">''House of Blades'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Myria]] is a location near the northwestern edge of the [[Latari Forest]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c14">''House of Blades'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Myria]] is a place from which [[Alin]]'s party originates.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17">''House of Blades'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Myria]] is a destination the people can be taken back to.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Myria]] is a destination [[Simon]]'s group intended to reach.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c22">''House of Blades'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Located near the [[Latari Forest]], Myria is a village protected by gates and watchmen. [[Alin]] previously entered the settlement through the [[Naraka Gate]], where he once used a simple cot, and [[Leah]] spent several years residing there. While [[Simon]] intended to liberate his friends from the village, he remained no different to [[Leah]] than he was to [[Alin]] or other locals. The village eventually faced devastation when it was raided by the [[Damascan army]] and attacked by an [[Endross Traveler]]. During these conflicts, battles between [[Naraka creatures]] and residents of the [[City of Light]] echoed through the area as a [[Naraka Incarnation]] fought upon its sands.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">12</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Myria]] is a village near the [[Latari Forest]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c1">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] intended to free his friends from [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c2">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Myria]] is a village.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] spent years in [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|In [[Myria]], [[Simon]] is the same person to [[Leah]] as he is to [[Alin]] or others in the village.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Myria]] has watchmen and gates.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] used a simple cot back in [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The back of the [[Damascan army]] might reach all the way to [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c17">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Myria]] is a location [[Alin]] entered via the [[Naraka Gate]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Myria]] is a village that was raided by [[Damascans]] and attacked by an [[Endross Traveler]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The battles between the [[City of Light]] residents and [[Naraka creatures]] echo in the village of [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Naraka Incarnation]] fought on the sand of [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== City of Light ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">3</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Myria]] was burned by the [[Naraka Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c4">''City of Light'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A girl [[Simon]] sees reminds him of [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Myria]] is a location where [[Alin]] once opened the [[Gates]] to [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|“Okay then,” Simon said, as soon as Alin and his hound had left. “You two can get everybody back to Myria on your own, can’t you?”<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Locations]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate locations]]
[[Category:Settlements]]
